2605.24562.title: PEDESTRIANQA: A Benchmark for Vision-Language Models on Pedestrian Intention and Trajectory Prediction
2605.24562.paper_summary: Pedestrian intention and trajectory prediction are critical for the safe deployment of autonomous driving systems, directly influencing navigation decisions in complex traffic environments. Recent advances in large vision-language models offer a powerful new paradigm for these tasks by combining high-capacity visual understanding with flexible natural language reasoning. In this work, we introduce PedestrianQA, a large-scale video-based dataset that formulates pedestrian intention and trajectory prediction as question-answering tasks augmented with structured rationales. PedestrianQA expresses richly annotated pedestrian sequences, in natural language, enabling VLMs to learn from visual dynamics, contextual cues, and interactions among traffic agents while generating concise explanations of their predictions without needing specialized architectures tailored for each task. Empirical evaluations across PIE, JAAD, TITAN, and IDD-PeD show that finetuning state-of-the-art VLMs on PedestrianQA significantly improves intention classification, trajectory forecasting accuracy, and the quality of explanatory rationales, demonstrating the strong potential of VLMs as a unified and explainable framework for safety-critical pedestrian behavior modeling.
2605.24562.paper_summary_zh: 行人意圖和軌跡預測對於自動駕駛系統的安全部署至關重要，直接影響在複雜交通環境中的導航決策。最近在大型視覺-語言模型方面的進展為這些任務提供了一種強大的新範式，通過結合高容量的視覺理解與靈活的自然語言推理。在這項工作中，我們介紹了PedestrianQA，一個大型視頻基礎數據集，將行人意圖和軌跡預測表述為增強了結構化推理的問答任務。PedestrianQA以自然語言表達豐富註釋的行人序列，使VLM能夠從視覺動態、上下文線索和交通代理之間的互動中學習，同時生成簡潔的預測解釋，而無需為每個任務量身定制專門的架構。在PIE、JAAD、TITAN和IDD-PeD的實證評估中，對PedestrianQA上最先進的VLM進行微調顯著提高了意圖分類、軌跡預測準確性和解釋性推理的質量，展示了VLM作為一個統一且可解釋的框架在安全關鍵的行為建模中的強大潛力。
2605.24562.author: Naman Mishra et.al.
2605.24562.authors: Naman Mishra, Shankar Gangisetty, C. V. Jawahar
